channel1_videos.title: Monica got a promotion, took steaks home, and was instantly fired 🥩
Phoebe actually bites her massage client and then gets sued for it

channel2_videos.title: Ted's boss proudly unveils his new skyscraper design, completely oblivious to its shape 🍆
Lily's plan to teach her boss a lesson almost cost Ted everything.

channel1_analysis.name: Friends Clips

worst_video.title: Phoebe actually bites her massage client and then gets sued for it

issues: Catastrophic failure on specific narrative titles: The 'Phoebe bites client' video getting 1 view is not just underperforming, it's a complete failure. This indicates either a broken upload, a universally unappealing clip, or a title/hook combination that actively repels viewers. The 'Monica got a promotion' title also significantly underperformed.
Extremely low engagement: Like-to-view ratio is poor (avg ~3% vs. industry standard 5-10% for Shorts). Comments are almost non-existent (0-1), signaling low audience connection and watch time.
Generic & overused titles/emojis: 'Character Being Character 😭' is bland, doesn't create a curiosity gap, and the emoji is diluted, limiting discoverability and click-through.
Inconsistent performance: While a baseline exists, the wild swings (1 view, 88 views) suggest a lack of understanding of what truly resonates, and the new title strategy is actively causing drops.
wins: Consistent posting frequency: The channel is active, which is a good foundation for testing and iteration.
Established niche: There's a clear audience for Friends clips, even if not fully tapped.

top_actions.action: IMMEDIATELY cease using the current 'narrative' title format that directly describes the clip's punchline or uses overly explicit emojis (e.g., 'Phoebe actually bites her massage client...').
top_actions.why: The 'Phoebe bites client' video (1 view) is a catastrophic failure. This title style either spoils the clip, uses off-putting imagery, or indicates a fundamental misunderstanding of Shorts hook psychology. The algorithm likely flags these as low-retention very quickly, killing the video before it gains any traction.
top_actions.expected_impact: Eliminate critical failures, preventing videos from dying at 1-3 views. This will stabilize overall channel performance and prevent negative signals to the algorithm, increasing views on these types of videos from 1-3 to at least the channel average (900+).

top_actions.action: Implement a 'curiosity gap' title strategy for ALL new uploads. Focus on a question, a surprising statement, or a cliffhanger that *doesn't* spoil the clip. Example: 'Phoebe's most awkward client encounter EVER' or 'Monica's promotion lasted less than an hour... here's why.'
top_actions.why: The current 'Character Being Character 😭' titles are generic and don't compel a swipe-up. A curiosity gap title forces the viewer to watch to get the answer, improving initial retention and click-through rate (CTR), which are crucial for algorithm distribution.
top_actions.expected_impact: Increase average views by 20-30% (from ~900 to 1100-1200) by improving CTR and initial watch time, leading to broader distribution.

top_actions.action: Add a clear, concise Call-to-Action (CTA) in the caption of every Short, e.g., 'Which Friends character is your favorite? 👇' or 'What's your favorite Monica moment? Let me know!'
top_actions.why: Current videos have almost no comments. Comments are a strong signal of engagement to the Shorts algorithm, indicating community and value, and can significantly boost a video's reach.
top_actions.expected_impact: Increase comment count per video from 0-1 to 3-5, boosting overall engagement signals and potentially increasing watch time % as viewers pause to comment.

channel2_analysis.name: HIMYM Clips

worst_video.title: Ted's boss proudly unveils his new skyscraper design, completely oblivious to its shape 🍆

issues: Critical failure on specific narrative titles: The 'Ted's boss 🍆' video getting 3 views is a catastrophic failure, mirroring the Friends channel's issue. This title style is actively harming performance.
Underperforming engagement: While views are good, the like-to-view ratio (avg ~0.8-1.5%) is still lower than optimal for viral Shorts (should aim for 3-5%+). Comments are also very low.
Over-reliance on generic titles: 'Barney's Schemes 😭' works, but it's not optimized for maximum virality. The emoji is overused and doesn't add value, leaving potential views on the table.
wins: Strong baseline performance: The channel consistently achieves 4k-26k views with its current generic titles, indicating strong audience interest in HIMYM clips and good clip selection.
Viral potential: Several videos hit 15k-26k views, showing the algorithm is willing to push this content when it resonates.
Consistent posting frequency: Good for maintaining algorithm favor and audience expectation.

top_actions.action: IMMEDIATELY cease using the current 'narrative' title format that directly describes the clip's punchline or uses overly explicit emojis (e.g., 'Ted's boss proudly unveils his new skyscraper design... 🍆').
top_actions.why: The 'Ted's boss 🍆' video (3 views) is a catastrophic failure, mirroring the Friends channel's issue. This title style actively repels viewers or signals low quality to the algorithm, killing videos before they gain traction.
top_actions.expected_impact: Eliminate critical failures, preventing videos from dying at 1-3 views. This will stabilize overall channel performance and prevent negative signals to the algorithm, increasing views on these types of videos from 3 to at least the channel average (11k+).

top_actions.action: Test a 'curiosity gap' title strategy for 50% of new uploads, while retaining the successful 'Barney's Schemes' format for the other 50%. Example: 'Ted's boss designed WHAT?!' or 'Lily's revenge plan almost ruined Ted's career.'
top_actions.why: While 'Barney's Schemes' works, it's not optimized for maximum virality. Introducing curiosity gap titles can significantly boost CTR and initial watch time, potentially pushing videos from 20k to 50k+ views. Testing allows for data-driven iteration without risking the current baseline.
top_actions.expected_impact: Increase average views by 15-25% (from ~11k to 13k-14k) on the tested videos, and identify new high-performing title formats, leading to higher overall channel growth.

top_actions.action: Implement a clear, engaging Call-to-Action (CTA) in the caption of every Short, e.g., 'What's your favorite Barney scheme? 👇' or 'Marshall & Lily are couple goals! Agree?'
top_actions.why: Despite high views, comments are almost non-existent. Driving comments is crucial for deeper engagement signals to the algorithm and building a community, which can extend video shelf life and reach.
top_actions.expected_impact: Increase comment count per video from 0-1 to 5-10, significantly boosting engagement signals and potentially leading to longer watch times as viewers interact.
